Notice Of Motion For Further Order Compelling Pltf'S Compliance And Sanctions; Request For Judicial Notice In Support;
Off Calendar Jul-18-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Jill Rizzo, Esq., for defendant and moving party Shorenstein Company, LLC, et al
Herbert Yanowitz, ,Esq., for Plaintiff and Opposing party Ruth Maiman

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: Defendants Shorenstein Company LLC, Shorenstein Realty Services' Motion For Further Order Compelling Plaintiff's Compliance And For Sanctions is granted as it seeks an order compelling further responses. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further within 10 days of notice of this order. Further, the motion is granted as it seeks additional monetary sanctions. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff and her counsel in the amount of $900, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. The motion is denied as it seeks evidentiary, issue, and terminating sanctions.

MODIFICATION: Motion is moot as further responses have been served - tentative otherwise is adopted.
COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff Ruth Maiman'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Jill Rizzo, Esq., appeared for Defendant & moving party: Shorenstein Co., LLC
Owen Noah, Esq., appeared for plaintiff & opposing party: Ruth Maiman

Ruling:
Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendants Shorenstein Company LLC, Shorenstein Realty Services LP's Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Further Responses To Special Interrogatories is granted. All responses must be full and complete at the time they are made. Plaintiff's responses to this discovery are inadequate. While the responses might convey the only information available at the time of the responses, the reservation that discovery is continuing is improper and makes the responses less than full and complete. Further responses shall be made within 30 days of notice of this order. The court finds that the responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $600, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT. MATTER NOT REPORTED
